ideas.title: How baking powder works and why it fails
ideas.userProblem: The reader needs to understand or diagnose baking powder that is not producing lift.
ideas.intendedAudience: Home cooks and bakers troubleshooting quick breads, cakes, or biscuits.
ideas.durability: The acid-base mechanism and freshness test are durable reference information.
ideas.productRelevance: Low; the answer does not require a product recommendation.
ideas.evidenceFeasibility: University extension sources directly cover the mechanism and freshness test.
ideas.competitionOpportunity: A concise mechanism-first guide can outperform thin expiration-only answers.
ideas.productionEffort: Low because the claims and safe diagnostic steps are bounded.
ideas.risk: Low when substitutions are kept recipe-specific and claims remain sourced.
notes: Migrated from the publicly released Handoff 1 article.

ideas.title: Why some practical answers are better than others
ideas.userProblem: The reader needs a transparent standard for judging the usefulness and evidence of an answer.
ideas.intendedAudience: Readers evaluating How Biscuit guidance and writers applying its editorial standard.
ideas.durability: Clear reasoning, traceability, useful next steps, and visible limits are durable principles.
ideas.productRelevance: None; this is an editorial standard rather than shopping guidance.
ideas.evidenceFeasibility: The standard is internally governed and supported by established writing guidance.
ideas.competitionOpportunity: Publishing the standard creates trust and a clear accountability surface.
ideas.productionEffort: Low because the article explains a bounded internal policy.
ideas.risk: Very low when it is labeled as an editorial standard rather than empirical research.
notes: Migrated from the publicly released Handoff 1 article.

ideas.title: Why salt melts ice and when it stops working
ideas.userProblem: The reader needs to de-ice a surface effectively without wasting salt or overstating temperature performance.
ideas.intendedAudience: Homeowners and renters managing winter ice on walks and steps.
ideas.durability: Freezing-point depression and chloride tradeoffs are durable scientific and practical topics.
ideas.productRelevance: Moderate because de-icing products exist, but the article makes no product recommendation.
ideas.evidenceFeasibility: Textbook chemistry and government environmental sources support the claims.
ideas.competitionOpportunity: A mechanism plus low-waste routine is more useful than a generic temperature chart.
ideas.productionEffort: Moderate because accessible math and safety context require careful rendering.
ideas.risk: Moderate because misuse can affect surfaces, pets, plants, and waterways.
notes: The trusted LaTeX source remains canonical.

briefs.researchQuestion: What triggers baking powder, what commonly prevents lift, and how can a reader test freshness safely?
briefs.userIntent: Understand the mechanism and decide whether the can or recipe process is the likely problem.
requiredClaims: baking powder contains acid and base
heat drives a second release in double-acting powder
prohibitedClaims: more powder always produces more height
baking powder and soda are interchangeable
testingNeeds: No first-hand test required; label the guide researched.
briefs.recommendation: Publish as a researched mechanism and troubleshooting guide.

briefs.researchQuestion: What minimum qualities make a practical answer useful, traceable, and honest about limits?
briefs.userIntent: Understand the editorial standard applied to How Biscuit answers.
exclusions: claims that the standard is universally optimal
requiredClaims: useful answers expose reasoning and limits
testingNeeds: Not applicable to an editorial standard.
safetyConsiderations: Label judgment as an editorial standard.
briefs.recommendation: Publish as a categoryless editorial standard.

briefs.researchQuestion: Why does salt melt ice, what limits sodium chloride, and what routine reduces waste?
briefs.userIntent: Understand the mechanism and clear bonded ice with the least practical chloride use.
requiredClaims: salt needs liquid water to form brine
prohibitedClaims: laboratory eutectic temperature guarantees field performance
testingNeeds: No first-hand product test required; retain researched label.
safetyConsiderations: Follow product labels and keep excess away from drains
briefs.recommendation: Keep the trusted LaTeX article with accessible KaTeX and MathML.
